Politics.headline: Trump fires Kristi Noem: what does it mean for ICE?
Politics.detail: Donald Trump has fired his controversial US homeland security secretary Kristi Noem, the public face.

Politics.headline: As Trump shows lack of direction on Iran, even Badenoch distances herself.
Politics.detail: Even Badenoch is distancing herself as Trump lacks direction regarding Iran.

Politics.headline: Canada's population shrank last year — a first for the country, StatsCan says.
Politics.detail: Statistics Canada reports that Canada's population experienced a decline last year, marking the first such decrease in the nation's history.

Politics.headline: Cryptocurrency and AI industries tested their influence in Illinois.
Politics.detail: The artificial intelligence and cryptocurrency industries have suffered a setback as they try to reshape the midterm elections and establish themselves as power players in American politics.

Politics.headline: Pope Leo tells broadcasters to reject Trumpian war propaganda.
Politics.detail: Pope Leo is urging broadcasters to resist war propaganda reminiscent of Trump's rhetoric.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Fed holds rates unchanged, expects higher inflation
Finance & Economy.detail: The US Federal Reserve held interest rates steady, projecting higher inflation and anticipating a single reduction in borrowing costs this year, as the impact of the US and Israeli war with Iran creates unusually high uncertainty for policymakers. Fed policymakers expect inflation to end the year at 2.7 percent, reflecting the fallout from the spike in global oil prices following the bombing campaign against Iran.

Finance & Economy.headline: IEA to consider release of more oil reserves as Iran war keeps prices high
Finance & Economy.detail: The International Energy Agency (IEA) is considering releasing more oil reserves as the Iran war continues to drive up prices. The conflict has disrupted global supply chains, particularly affecting Africa, and has led to increased prices for oil, gas, shipping, and air freight.

Finance & Economy.headline: U.S. Fed keeps interest rate unchanged at 3.5-3.75 pct
Finance & Economy.detail: The U.S. Federal Reserve decided to maintain its target range for the federal funds rate at 3.5-3.75 percent, in line with market expectations, while indicators suggest economic activity is expanding at a solid pace. Uncertainty about the economic outlook remains elevated due to the implications of Middle East developments for the U.S. economy.

Finance & Economy.headline: Global race as market for high-grade iron ore grows
Finance & Economy.detail: Global demand for high-grade iron ores is increasing as the steel sector decarbonises, creating significant opportunities for new suppliers, including Australia. With direct reduction (DR) emerging as the leading pathway for steel sector decarbonisation, a forecast supply deficit for DR-grade iron ore signals an opportunity for new suppliers.

Finance & Economy.headline: GDP data shows a growing economy
Finance & Economy.detail: Economic data released today confirms the New Zealand economy was growing at the end of last year, with real Gross Domestic Product rising 0.2% in the December quarter. Before the recent conflict in the Middle East, economic forecasters had been predicting growth to continue and strengthen in 2026.

Technology.headline: AI Glasses Shift Into Momentum Mode, Shipments Grow 322% in 2025
Technology.detail: AI glasses shipments soared 322% in 2025 to 8.7 million units as advances in multimodal AI and lighter wearable design propelled the market.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Warns of Hackers Supercharging Cyberattacks With AI
Technology.detail: AI is becoming a force multiplier for cybercriminals, helping threat actors automate phishing, malware development, and reconnaissance while accelerating the pace and scale of cyberattacks.

Technology.headline: Okta made a nightmare micromanager for your AI agents
Technology.detail: Where are you? What are you working on? Why are you doing that?

Technology.headline: Google overhauls its Maps app, adding in more AI features to help people get around
Technology.detail: Google is adding more AI features to its Maps app to help people navigate and explore the world around them.

Technology.headline: State snoops and spyware vendors planting info-stealing malware on iPhones, Google warns
Technology.detail: Darksword is the second iOS exploit chain in a month.

Science.headline: Tapping into the science of maple syrup
Science.detail: MPR News host Angela Davis discusses the science behind maple syrup, how climate change is affecting the sap season in Minnesota, and what trees may better withstand climate change with guests Laura Irish-Hanson and Brandon Miller.

Science.headline: When will we get back to the Moon?
Science.detail: NASA's Artemis II mission is scheduled to launch in April 2026 for a fly around the moon, paving the way for future lunar landings, with other countries like China also aiming to land on the lunar surface.

Science.headline: Astrophysicist Breaks Down Science in Project Hail Mary
Science.detail: An astrophysicist reviews the film adaptation of Andy Weir's 'Project Hail Mary,' commenting on the science and fiction aspects of the plot, including the possibility of organisms spreading between solar systems and the challenges of interstellar travel.

Science.headline: Work Arrangements for Employees Affected by the Strike on 19 March 2026 (Memo # UHR2526-037)
Science.detail: Due to a nationwide transportation strike, Ateneo de Manila University announced that undergraduate and graduate classes for various schools will be held online on March 19, 2026, with faculty able to opt for on-site classes after consulting with students.

Science.headline: Artemis II is ready to launch
Science.detail: NASA's Artemis II mission plans to fly around the Moon and back this April with four astronauts, paving the way for humans to once again place their boots on the Moon.

Health.headline: GLP-1s Tied to Lower Risk of Psychiatric Decline
Health.detail: Recent findings suggest a link between GLP-1 medications and a reduced risk of psychiatric decline.

Health.headline: Frequent brisk activity sessions are linked to better brain health and executive function
Health.detail: Engaging in frequent, brisk physical activity is associated with improved brain health and executive function.

Health.headline: Surgery plays a central role in treatment for colorectal cancer – and is usually the first step
Health.detail: Surgery is a primary and often initial treatment approach for colorectal cancer, according to Mayo Clinic experts.

Health.headline: Researchers identify two gut bacteria that can produce serotonin, a key chemical that regulates bowel movements.
Health.detail: Two types of gut bacteria capable of producing serotonin, a crucial chemical for regulating bowel movements, have been identified by researchers.

Health.headline: WHO international scheme to evaluate household water treatment technologies: results of Rounds III and...
Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) has released results from Round III of its international scheme to evaluate household water treatment technologies.

Sports.headline: WADA delays decision over potential ban of Trump from major sporting events until after World Cup
Sports.detail: The World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) has postponed its decision regarding a potential ban on Donald Trump from major sporting events until after the World Cup. The delay is to allow for further investigation into alleged doping violations.

Sports.headline: Eagles agreed to terms on a one-year contract with TE Dallas Goedert.
Sports.detail: The Eagles and Dallas Goedert have agreed to a one-year contract extension after working to postpone the void date on Goedert's expiring contract twice in the last week, ensuring his presence on the team for another year.

Sports.headline: Venezuela hero Eugenio Suarez taps faith after shocking Team USA: 'All the glory is for the Lord Jesus'
Sports.detail: Eugenio Suarez credits his faith after Venezuela's victory over Team USA in the World Baseball Classic.

Sports.headline: Senegal stripped of Africa Cup of Nations title two months after final, Morocco declared champions
Sports.detail: CAF stripped Senegal of their Africa Cup of Nations title and declared Morocco the champions, two months after the final match. The reasons for this decision are currently unclear.

Sports.headline: How Shohei Ohtani Fared in First 2026 Dodgers Spring Training Start
Sports.detail: Shohei Ohtani made his first start for the Dodgers in spring training 2026. The four-time MVP returned to the mound on Wednesday.

Entertainment.headline: Oscars 2026: Kiwis clean up with major awards, 'One Battle After Another' reigns supreme
Entertainment.detail: New Zealanders have taken home major awards at the Oscars, with 'One Battle After Another' winning Best Picture.

Entertainment.headline: Ryan Gosling Is Down for a Harry Styles Musical Team Up After 'SNL' Cameos!
Entertainment.detail: Ryan Gosling expressed enthusiasm for collaborating with Harry Styles on a musical project following their respective appearances on 'Saturday Night Live'.

Entertainment.headline: Taylor Frankie Paul Confirms Reports 'SLOMW' on Pause Amid Domestic Violence Investigation.
Entertainment.detail: Taylor Frankie Paul has confirmed that 'Secret Lives of Mormon Wives' is on pause due to a domestic violence investigation.

Entertainment.headline: Miley Cyrus on whether she would ever headline Super Bowl
Entertainment.detail: Miley Cyrus comments on the possibility of her headlining the Super Bowl in the future.

Entertainment.headline: Nora Fatehi claims she was 'misled' about vulgar 'Sarke Chunar' lyrics, alleges use of AI: 'I warned KD the Devil makers'
Entertainment.detail: Nora Fatehi alleges she was misled regarding vulgar lyrics in 'Sarke Chunar' and claims AI was used, stating she warned the makers of 'KD the Devil'.

Crime & Justice.headline: Two men charged with National Security Act offence
Crime & Justice.detail: Nematollah Shahsavani and Alireza Farasati have been charged with engaging in conduct likely to assist a foreign intelligence service, specifically Iran, between July 9, 2025, and August 15, 2025, including gathering information and undertaking reconnaissance of targets in the UK. They are due to appear at Westminster Magistrates' Court on Thursday, 19 March 2026.

Crime & Justice.headline: Minister Anandasangaree in Toronto to highlight new tools to help law enforcement investigate threats and keep Canadians safe
Crime & Justice.detail: The Honourable Gary Anandasangaree, Minister of Public Safety, and Myron Demkiw, Chief of Toronto Police Service, will hold a press conference on Canada's efforts to support law enforcement agencies and the Canadian Security Intelligence Service (CSIS) with new tools to investigate serious threats, disrupt organized crime, and protect communities. The press conference will be held on Thursday, March 19, 2026, at the Toronto Police Service Headquarters.

Crime & Justice.headline: Deputy President Paul Mashatile responds to oral questions in the National Assembly, 19 Mar
Crime & Justice.detail: Deputy President Paul Mashatile will respond to Oral Questions in the National Assembly on Thursday, March 19, to engage on key governance priorities, policy matters and service delivery issues. Mashatile will also reiterate the Justice, Crime Prevention and Security (JCPS) Cabinet committee's commitment to improving inter-departmental coordination, enhancing intelligence-led policing, and modernizing the Criminal Justice System in response to the increase in violent crime.

Crime & Justice.headline: Newly discovered Whitey Bulger writings show former FBI agent was framed, lawyers allege
Crime & Justice.detail: Attorneys for former FBI agent John Connolly have filed a new motion seeking to overturn his murder conviction, citing newly disclosed evidence tied to mobster James “Whitey” Bulger.

Crime & Justice.headline: Utah woman who wrote a book on grief after husband's death found guilty of murdering him.
Crime & Justice.detail: Kouri Richins, a Utah woman who wrote a children's book about grief after her husband's death, has been found guilty of his murder. She was accused of killing him with a fentanyl-laced Moscow mule in 2022 after previously attempting to poison him.

Education.headline: New Zealand-China education ties 'at risk of faltering'
Education.detail: Educational ties between New Zealand and China face challenges, potentially impacting international education.

Education.headline: Kent begins vaccine roll-out as meningitis cases spread
Education.detail: The University of Kent has started a vaccine rollout following an outbreak of meningitis among students.

Education.headline: Judge strikes down Arkansas law mandating schools display the Ten Commandments
Education.detail: A judge has overturned an Arkansas law that required schools to display the Ten Commandments.

Education.headline: College Republicans sue University of Florida's president over deactivation of its chapter
Education.detail: The College Republicans at the University of Florida are suing the university's president after the group's chapter was deactivated.

Education.headline: Business school 'lacked resources' to deliver promised courses
Education.detail: A business school reportedly lacked the necessary resources to provide the courses it had promised.

Lifestyle.headline: Dream vacation becomes nightmare as dozens fall ill in suspected norovirus outbreak on cruise ship
Lifestyle.detail: Nearly 80 people fell ill with suspected norovirus on Holland America's Westerdam cruise ship during visit to Hong Kong, prompting a health investigation.

Lifestyle.headline: Brain aging may accelerate after cancer treatment, study suggests
Lifestyle.detail: New research suggests childhood cancer survivors may experience accelerated biological aging from treatments, potentially affecting memory and cognition.

Lifestyle.headline: Aldi recalls popular snack food over possible rodent hair contamination.
Lifestyle.detail: Aldi has issued a recall for a popular snack food due to the potential presence of rodent hair.

Lifestyle.headline: Chocolate candy sold at Lidl recalled over undeclared hazelnut allergen
Lifestyle.detail: A chocolate candy product sold at Lidl has been recalled because it contains undeclared hazelnut allergens.

Lifestyle.headline: Shilpa Shetty Reveals Her 'Secret To Staying Calm' Before Work
Lifestyle.detail: Shilpa Shetty shares her secret to maintaining calmness before starting her workday.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on Rachel Reeves and the EU: the right ambition is held back by outdated red lines.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discusses Rachel Reeves' ambition regarding the EU but suggests it's constrained by outdated red lines.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on the legacy of Jürgen Habermas: philosophical sustenance for illiberal times.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial reflects on Jürgen Habermas' legacy and its philosophical relevance in illiberal times.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on the fragile politics of the union: Labour's woes have consequences beyond Westminster.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial board discusses the fragile state of political unions, highlighting how Labour's struggles extend beyond Westminster.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on falling donations to charity: rising living costs are part of the problem, but not all of it.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This piece examines the issue of declining charitable donations, pointing to rising living costs as a contributing factor, but not the sole cause.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Proposed changes to Transgender Persons Act turn back the clock on rights and dignity.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Indian Express discusses proposed changes to the Transgender Persons Act and how it could negatively impact rights.

World.headline: Iran and Israel trade strikes on energy facilities in worsening war
World.detail: Iran and Israel traded strikes on key energy facilities in the Middle East, complicating U.S. efforts to curb turmoil in markets as the conflict stretched toward three weeks. Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City suffered "extensive damage” after an Iranian attack.

World.headline: U.S. forces bomb Iranian missile sites near Strait of Hormuz
World.detail: U.S. forces struck Iranian missile sites along Iran's coastline near the Strait of Hormuz, the U.S. Central Command said. The U.S. forces successfully employed multiple 5,000-pound deep penetrator munitions on hardened Iranian missile sites.

World.headline: Israeli air force strikes major Iranian natural gas facility in Bushehr
World.detail: The Israeli air force has struck Iran's major natural gas facility, located in Bushehr in the south of the country, several Israeli media outlets reported. Israel's state-owned Kan TV News reported that the strike was carried out in coordination with the U.S. administration.

World.headline: Social media impacts youth wellbeing: U.N. happiness report
World.detail: Heavy social media use is undermining youth wellbeing in many countries, according to a U.N.-sponsored report. This year's World Happiness Report highlighted the impact of social media as many countries impose or mull legislative restrictions on social media use for young people.

World.headline: Xinhua world news summary at 0030 GMT, March 19
World.detail: Chadian President Mahamat Idriss Deby Itno convened an emergency security meeting on Wednesday evening following a deadly drone attack along the Chad-Sudan border and ordered the "complete closure" of the border. The attack on Wednesday caused "several Chadian casualties," according to the statement.

Zimbabwe.headline: Heavy rains kill over 130 in Zimbabwe
Zimbabwe.detail: At least 132 people have died and 72 others have been injured due to heavy rains across Zimbabwe since October, damaging infrastructure and triggering water-borne diseases.

Zimbabwe.headline: Fuel prices surge in Zimbabwe
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabweans are facing rising fuel costs after fresh price hikes announced by the Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority, influenced by tightening global crude oil markets.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZANU PF MP Accuses Hulengende, Malloti Of Corrupting Young People
Zimbabwe.detail: MP Malinganiso has accused Malloti and Hulengende of promoting drugs and substance abuse, raising concerns about social media regulation.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim intensifies UN campaign
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is increasing its diplomatic efforts to secure a non-permanent seat on the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) before the upcoming elections.

Zimbabwe.headline: Goverment diversifies fuel arteries as Middle East tensions rise
Zimbabwe.detail: In response to rising tensions in the Middle East, the government is taking steps to diversify fuel supply routes.
